title: Ignition Failure
shortDescription: The unit failed to ignite after the maximum number of attempts.
fullDescription:
Error code 02 on Rinnai tankless water heaters indicates the unit attempted to ignite but failed to detect a flame. The igniter sparks but no flame is established within the allowed time window.

This is one of the most common Rinnai error codes and is frequently caused by a closed gas valve, insufficient gas pressure, or a dirty igniter. On LP gas models, an empty or low propane tank is a common culprit.

If the unit was recently installed, air in the gas line must be purged before the unit can ignite. It may take several attempts to clear the air from the line.
safetyWarning: This error involves the gas ignition system. If you smell gas at any time, do not try to ignite the unit. Evacuate the area and call your gas company immediately.

causes.cause: Empty or low propane tank (LP models)
Air in gas line after installation

symptoms: Clicking or sparking sound with no ignition
Unit attempts to start then shuts off

fixSteps.title: Check Gas Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the gas shutoff valve is fully open and other gas appliances are working. For LP, check tank level.

fixSteps.title: Reset the Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the unit off using the power button, wait 10 seconds, and turn it back on. Try running hot water again.

fixSteps.title: Clean the Igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front cover and gently clean the igniter electrode and flame rod with fine sandpaper.

fixSteps.title: Purge Gas Line
fixSteps.instruction: If newly installed, open a hot water faucet for 2-3 minutes to cycle the unit and purge air from the gas line.

whenToCallPro: If gas supply is confirmed and cleaning the igniter does not resolve the issue, call a licensed plumber to check gas pressure and internal components.

faq.q: How do I reset a Rinnai after error 02?
faq.a: Press the power button to turn off, wait 10 seconds, then press it again to restart.

faq.q: Can cold weather cause error 02?
faq.a: Extreme cold can reduce LP gas vaporization rates, causing insufficient pressure. A tank blanket may help.

title: Bath Fill Shut-Off
shortDescription: The bath fill function shut off after reaching the set water volume.
fullDescription:
Error code 03 on Rinnai units indicates the bath fill function has completed or shut off. On models with a bath fill feature, this code appears when the programmed volume of hot water has been delivered.

This is typically an informational status code rather than a fault. It indicates normal operation of the bath fill timer or volume counter. The unit will resume normal operation when the next hot water demand is detected.

If this code appears unexpectedly or the bath fill feature is not being used, it may indicate a control board issue or incorrect programming.

fixSteps.title: Reset the Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Press the power button to turn the unit off, wait a few seconds, and turn it back on to clear the bath fill status.

fixSteps.title: Check Bath Fill Settings
fixSteps.instruction: Review the bath fill settings on the controller. Adjust the volume or disable the feature if not needed.

fixSteps.title: Verify Normal Operation
fixSteps.instruction: Open a hot water faucet to confirm the unit heats water normally after resetting.

whenToCallPro: If error 03 appears repeatedly without using the bath fill feature, contact Rinnai support or a technician.

faq.q: Is error 03 a real problem?
faq.a: Usually no. It is a status indicator for the bath fill feature, not a malfunction.

faq.q: How do I disable the bath fill feature?
faq.a: Refer to your specific model's owner manual for instructions on disabling bath fill settings.

title: Exhaust Temperature Limit Exceeded
shortDescription: The exhaust temperature has exceeded the safe operating limit.
fullDescription:
Error code 05 indicates the exhaust gas temperature has exceeded the maximum safe threshold. The exhaust thermistor detects temperatures above the limit, and the unit shuts down to prevent damage to the venting system and potential safety hazards.

Excessive exhaust temperature can be caused by restricted exhaust venting, a dirty heat exchanger that is not transferring heat efficiently to the water, or a combustion air supply issue. Scale buildup on the heat exchanger forces the burner to work harder, resulting in higher exhaust temperatures.

This error should be taken seriously as it can indicate a combustion efficiency problem that may also produce elevated carbon monoxide levels.
safetyWarning: High exhaust temperatures may indicate incomplete combustion and elevated carbon monoxide levels. Ensure CO detectors are working. Do not override this safety shutdown.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Exhaust Vent
fixSteps.instruction: Check the entire exhaust vent run and termination for blockages, disconnections, or restrictions.

fixSteps.title: Check Air Intake
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the air intake vent is not blocked and has adequate clearance from obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Descale Heat Exchanger
fixSteps.instruction: Flush the heat exchanger with descaling solution using isolation valves and a pump for 45-60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Reset and Monitor
fixSteps.instruction: After addressing the cause, reset the unit and monitor exhaust temperature if a display is available.

whenToCallPro: If the venting is clear and the heat exchanger has been flushed but the error persists, a technician should perform a combustion analysis.

faq.q: Can a dirty heat exchanger cause high exhaust temperature?
faq.a: Yes. Scale reduces heat transfer to the water, so more heat exits through the exhaust.

faq.q: How often should I descale my Rinnai?
faq.a: Annually in areas with hard water, or every 2-3 years in areas with soft water.

title: Exhaust Blockage / Air Supply Issue
shortDescription: The unit has detected a blockage in the venting or air supply system.
fullDescription:
Error code 10 on Rinnai tankless water heaters indicates an issue with the exhaust venting or combustion air supply. The fan current or air pressure sensor detects conditions outside the normal range, signaling a potential blockage.

This is a very common error code that can be caused by a bird nest in the vent, ice buildup on the vent termination, disconnected vent pipes, or a failed combustion fan. In some installations, high winds can also trigger this error intermittently.

Proper venting is essential for safe operation. Any restriction in the exhaust path or combustion air supply can lead to dangerous carbon monoxide accumulation.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: A blocked vent can cause carbon monoxide to enter the living space. Ensure CO detectors are working. If a CO alarm sounds, evacuate immediately and call 911.

causes.cause: Bird nest or debris in exhaust vent
Ice or snow blocking vent termination
High wind conditions at vent termination

symptoms: Unit shuts down and will not restart

fixSteps.title: Inspect Vent Termination
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and check the exhaust and intake vent terminations for blockages, ice, nests, or debris.

fixSteps.title: Check Full Vent Run
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the vent pipe from the unit to the termination for disconnections, sags, or damage.

fixSteps.title: Listen for Fan
fixSteps.instruction: When the unit attempts to start, listen for the combustion fan. If there is no sound, the fan may have failed.

fixSteps.title: Reset and Test
fixSteps.instruction: After clearing any blockage, reset the unit and run hot water to test operation.

whenToCallPro: If the vent is clear but the error persists, call a technician to test the combustion fan and pressure sensor.

faq.q: Why does error 10 happen more in winter?
faq.a: Condensate from the exhaust can freeze at the vent termination in sub-freezing temperatures, creating a blockage.

faq.q: Can wind cause error 10?
faq.a: Yes. Strong gusts can push exhaust back into the unit. A wind-resistant vent cap may help.

title: No Ignition - Repeated Failure
shortDescription: The unit has failed to ignite after the maximum number of consecutive attempts.
fullDescription:
Error code 11 on Rinnai units indicates repeated ignition failure over multiple cycles. While error 02 occurs on a single failed attempt, error 11 indicates the unit has been unable to ignite across multiple consecutive demand cycles.

This is a more persistent ignition problem than error 02 and often indicates a component failure rather than a temporary condition. The igniter, gas valve solenoid, or control board may need attention.

The unit will lock out with this error and may require a manual reset to attempt ignition again.
safetyWarning: Repeated ignition failures may cause unburned gas accumulation. If you smell gas, evacuate and call your gas company. Do not repeatedly attempt to restart.

fixSteps.title: Verify Gas Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm gas is reaching the unit by checking the shutoff valve and testing other gas appliances.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the unit off, unplug it for 60 seconds, plug back in, and turn on. This performs a full reset.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front cover and check the igniter electrode for excessive wear, cracking, or heavy carbon deposits.

fixSteps.title: Schedule Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the error returns after reset, schedule professional service. Do not continue to attempt restarts.

whenToCallPro: If the unit will not ignite after one power cycle and gas supply is confirmed, call a licensed Rinnai technician for ignition system diagnosis.

faq.q: What is the difference between error 02 and 11?
faq.a: Error 02 is a single ignition failure. Error 11 indicates repeated failures across multiple attempts, suggesting a more serious issue.

faq.q: Can I fix error 11 myself?
faq.a: You can check gas supply and reset the unit, but persistent error 11 usually requires professional service.

title: Flame Failure During Operation
shortDescription: The flame was lost while the unit was actively heating water.
fullDescription:
Error code 12 indicates the Rinnai unit successfully ignited but the flame was lost during burner operation. The flame detection rod stopped sensing a flame while the gas valve was open.

This differs from error 02/11 (failure to ignite) because the flame was initially established. Common causes include gas pressure fluctuations, a dirty flame rod, wind effects on the venting system, or condensate dripping on the burner.

If this error occurs intermittently during heavy use, it may indicate the gas line is undersized and cannot maintain pressure when multiple gas appliances are operating simultaneously.
safetyWarning: Flame loss during operation can indicate unstable gas supply. If you smell gas, shut off the supply and call your gas company.

fixSteps.title: Clean Flame Rod
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front cover and clean the flame detection rod with fine sandpaper or emery cloth to remove carbon buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check Condensate Drain
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the condensate drain is not clogged and condensate is not backing up onto the burner.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Venting for Drafts
fixSteps.instruction: Check the vent termination for wind exposure and verify all vent joints are sealed.

fixSteps.title: Monitor Gas Pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Have a technician measure inlet gas pressure while the unit fires at full capacity to check for pressure drops.

whenToCallPro: If flame rod cleaning does not resolve the issue, a technician should perform a full gas pressure test and combustion analysis.

faq.q: Why does my hot water go cold and then come back?
faq.a: The unit loses flame, shuts down, and then reignites on the next demand cycle. Cleaning the flame rod usually helps.

faq.q: How often should the flame rod be cleaned?
faq.a: At least once a year during annual maintenance.

title: Thermal Fuse Activated
shortDescription: The thermal fuse has blown due to overheating, shutting down the unit as a safety measure.
fullDescription:
Error code 14 indicates the thermal fuse inside the Rinnai unit has activated (blown) due to excessive heat. The thermal fuse is a one-time safety device that permanently opens when it detects temperatures above its rated threshold.

This is a serious error that indicates the unit experienced dangerously high temperatures. The cause must be identified and resolved before the thermal fuse is replaced. Common causes include combustion problems, blocked heat exchanger, or a failed temperature control.

Once a thermal fuse blows, it cannot be reset and must be physically replaced. This is a safety device that should never be bypassed.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: The thermal fuse activates to prevent fire and equipment damage. Do not bypass it. The root cause of overheating must be identified and corrected before replacing the fuse.

causes.cause: Blocked or scaled heat exchanger causing overheating
Failed gas modulation valve stuck open

symptoms: Cannot be reset by power cycling

fixSteps.title: Do Not Reset Repeatedly
fixSteps.instruction: This error requires professional service. Do not attempt to bypass the thermal fuse.

fixSteps.title: Turn Off Gas
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the gas supply to the unit until a technician can inspect it.

fixSteps.title: Check Water Flow
fixSteps.instruction: Verify that water valves are fully open and the inlet filter is not clogged.

fixSteps.title: Schedule Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: Contact a licensed Rinnai technician to diagnose the overheating cause and replace the thermal fuse.

whenToCallPro: Immediately. Error 14 always requires professional diagnosis and repair.

faq.q: Can I replace the thermal fuse myself?
faq.a: While the fuse itself is inexpensive, the root cause of overheating must be diagnosed professionally before replacement.

faq.q: How much does thermal fuse replacement cost?
faq.a: The fuse is $5-$15, but professional diagnosis and repair typically costs $200-$500 total.

title: Overheating - Outlet Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The outlet water temperature has exceeded the safe operating limit.
fullDescription:
Error code 15 on Rinnai tankless water heaters indicates the outlet water temperature has exceeded the maximum safe limit, typically around 185 degrees Fahrenheit. The unit shuts down to prevent scalding and heat exchanger damage.

Low water flow through the unit is the most common cause, often due to a clogged inlet filter, partially closed valves, or heavy scale buildup in the heat exchanger. When water flows too slowly through the heat exchanger, it absorbs too much heat.

This error requires attention to prevent heat exchanger damage from repeated overheating events.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: Water above 150 degrees Fahrenheit causes severe scalding burns in seconds. Do not use hot water until this error is resolved.

fixSteps.title: Clean Inlet Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate and remove the inlet water filter. Clean it thoroughly under running water to remove sediment.

fixSteps.title: Check Water Valves
fixSteps.instruction: Verify both inlet and outlet isolation valves are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Descale Heat Exchanger
fixSteps.instruction: Connect a descaling pump and flush the heat exchanger with vinegar or commercial descaling solution for 45-60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test Flow Rate
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, measure the hot water flow rate. It should meet the minimum activation flow for your model.

whenToCallPro: If descaling and filter cleaning do not resolve the overheating, a technician should check the flow control valve and gas modulation.

faq.q: How do I prevent overheating?
faq.a: Maintain the unit annually with descaling and filter cleaning. Install a water softener if you have hard water.

faq.q: Can I still use the unit after error 15?
faq.a: Wait for the unit to cool, then address the cause before resuming use.

title: Overheating - Heat Exchanger Too Hot
shortDescription: The heat exchanger temperature has exceeded the maximum safe threshold.
fullDescription:
Error code 16 indicates the heat exchanger itself has reached a temperature beyond its safe operating limit. This is measured by the heat exchanger thermistor and is distinct from error 15 which measures outlet water temperature.

This error is often more serious than error 15 because it indicates the heat exchanger body is overheating, which can cause permanent damage including warping or cracking. The most common cause is severely restricted water flow or a badly scaled heat exchanger.

Repeated triggering of error 16 can lead to premature heat exchanger failure, which is an expensive repair.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: An overheated heat exchanger can crack, causing water and gas leaks. Turn off the unit and let it cool before any inspection.

causes.cause: Severe scale buildup in heat exchanger

symptoms: Possible rumbling or boiling sound from unit

fixSteps.title: Let Unit Cool
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit and wait at least 30 minutes for the heat exchanger to cool down.

fixSteps.title: Check Water Flow
fixSteps.instruction: Verify valves are open, clean the inlet filter, and check for any restrictions in the plumbing.

fixSteps.title: Perform Full Descale
fixSteps.instruction: Flush the heat exchanger with a descaling pump and solution for at least 60 minutes. This is essential.

fixSteps.title: Professional Inspection
fixSteps.instruction: After descaling, have a technician verify heat exchanger integrity and thermistor function.

whenToCallPro: If descaling does not resolve the issue, or if error 16 has occurred multiple times, professional inspection is needed to check for heat exchanger damage.

faq.q: Can a heat exchanger be repaired?
faq.a: Generally no. If cracked or severely damaged, the entire heat exchanger must be replaced.

faq.q: How much does a Rinnai heat exchanger cost?
faq.a: Typically $500-$1,500 installed, depending on the model. Check warranty coverage first.

title: Electrical Grounding Fault
shortDescription: The unit has detected an electrical grounding problem.
fullDescription:
Error code 19 indicates the Rinnai unit has detected an electrical grounding fault. Proper grounding is essential for the safe operation of the unit's electronic controls and for the flame detection system to work correctly.

The flame detection circuit relies on a proper ground reference to detect the flame signal. Without adequate grounding, the unit may not be able to detect flame presence even when the burner is lit, leading to ignition failures.

This error often occurs in homes with older electrical systems or improper outlet wiring. It can also be caused by a faulty power cord or internal wiring issue.
safetyWarning: Electrical grounding issues can be a shock hazard. Do not touch the unit with wet hands. Have a qualified electrician verify proper grounding.

causes.cause: Loose ground wire in the unit

symptoms: Other error codes may also appear

fixSteps.title: Check Outlet Grounding
fixSteps.instruction: Use an outlet tester to verify the power outlet has proper grounding and correct polarity.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Power Cord
fixSteps.instruction: Check the power cord for damage and ensure the ground prong is intact.

fixSteps.title: Try a Different Outlet
fixSteps.instruction: If available, plug the unit into a different properly grounded outlet to test.

fixSteps.title: Check Internal Ground
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the internal ground wire is securely connected.

whenToCallPro: If the outlet tests properly but the error persists, call an electrician to check grounding. If the internal ground is the issue, call a Rinnai technician.

faq.q: Can I use an extension cord with my Rinnai?
faq.a: No. Rinnai units should be plugged directly into a properly grounded outlet.

faq.q: What is reversed polarity?
faq.a: It means the hot and neutral wires at the outlet are swapped. An outlet tester can detect this.

title: Condensate Drain Blockage
shortDescription: The condensate drain system is blocked or not draining properly.
fullDescription:
Error code 25 on Rinnai condensing tankless water heaters indicates the condensate drain is blocked or the condensate level sensor has triggered. Condensing units extract additional heat from exhaust gases, producing acidic condensate that must drain away freely.

When the condensate cannot drain, it can back up into the heat exchanger and cause corrosion, reduced efficiency, and eventual component damage. The drain line, trap, or neutralizer (if installed) may be clogged.

This error only applies to condensing models (typically those with an efficiency rating of 95% or higher).
symptoms: Water visible around the unit base

fixSteps.title: Clear Drain Line
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the condensate drain line and clear any blockage by flushing with warm water or using a small brush.

fixSteps.title: Check Condensate Neutralizer
fixSteps.instruction: If a condensate neutralizer is installed, check if the media is exhausted or the housing is clogged. Replace media if needed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Drain Trap
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the condensate trap and verify it is properly installed with the correct water seal.

fixSteps.title: Reset and Verify
fixSteps.instruction: After clearing the drain, reset the unit and run hot water. Verify condensate flows freely from the drain.

whenToCallPro: If the drain is clear and the error persists, the condensate level sensor may need replacement.

faq.q: Does my Rinnai produce condensate?
faq.a: Only condensing models (RU series and others rated 95%+ efficiency) produce significant condensate.

faq.q: What is a condensate neutralizer?
faq.a: A device containing calcium carbonate media that neutralizes the acidic condensate before it enters the drain system.

title: Condensate Drain Overflow
shortDescription: The condensate has overflowed beyond the normal drain path.
fullDescription:
Error code 29 is related to error 25 and indicates a more severe condensate drainage problem where the condensate level has risen beyond the primary drain path. This may trigger after error 25 if the blockage is not cleared.

This error means condensate is at risk of entering the combustion chamber or spilling onto the floor. The unit will not operate until the condensate has been drained and the blockage cleared.

In severe cases, condensate backup can corrode internal components and cause water damage to walls and flooring beneath the unit.

causes.cause: Frozen drain line in cold weather

fixSteps.title: Clean Up Standing Water
fixSteps.instruction: Mop up any water around the unit to prevent damage. Place a bucket under the drain outlet.

fixSteps.title: Clear Drain Line
fixSteps.instruction: Clear the condensate drain line of all blockages. Flush thoroughly with warm water.

fixSteps.title: Check Drain Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the condensate drain line is properly connected to the unit and routes to an approved drain location.

fixSteps.title: Reset and Monitor
fixSteps.instruction: Reset the unit and monitor the condensate drain during operation to confirm proper flow.

whenToCallPro: If the drain is clear but condensate continues to overflow, a technician should check for internal condensate path issues.

faq.q: Can condensate damage my floor?
faq.a: Yes. Condensate is mildly acidic and can damage flooring and drywall over time if not properly drained.

faq.q: Should I install a condensate pump?
faq.a: If gravity drainage is not possible, a condensate pump is required. Ensure it is rated for acidic condensate.

title: Inlet Water Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The cold water inlet temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription:
Error code 31 indicates the cold water inlet temperature sensor (thermistor) is providing readings outside the acceptable range or has failed entirely. This sensor measures the incoming water temperature so the control board can calculate the required temperature rise.

Without accurate inlet temperature data, the unit cannot properly modulate the burner to achieve the set output temperature. The sensor may have failed open, short, or have a corroded connection.

This is typically a straightforward sensor replacement repair.

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the inlet temperature sensor connector is firmly seated on the control board.

fixSteps.title: Measure Sensor Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. Compare to the service manual specification for current water temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If resistance is out of range, replace the inlet temperature sensor with an OEM Rinnai part.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not clear the error, the control board input circuit may be faulty.

faq.q: How do I know which sensor to order?
faq.a: Reference your Rinnai model number on the rating plate. Different models use different sensor part numbers.

faq.q: Can I use a universal thermistor?
faq.a: No. Use the OEM Rinnai sensor to ensure the correct resistance characteristics for your model.

title: Outlet Water Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The hot water outlet temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription:
Error code 32 indicates the outlet water temperature sensor is providing abnormal readings. This sensor measures the temperature of hot water leaving the unit and is critical for the temperature control loop.

If this sensor fails, the control board cannot verify the output water temperature matches the set point. The unit shuts down as a safety precaution to prevent delivering water at an uncontrolled temperature.

Like the inlet sensor, this is typically a straightforward replacement, but the root cause should be identified as repeated sensor failures may indicate an underlying issue.
safetyWarning: A failed outlet sensor could theoretically allow the unit to deliver dangerously hot water. Do not bypass this sensor.

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the outlet temperature sensor connector is securely plugged in at the control board.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and measure resistance with a multimeter. Compare to the service manual specification.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If out of specification, replace with an OEM Rinnai outlet temperature sensor.

fixSteps.title: Verify After Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Run hot water and confirm the displayed outlet temperature matches a thermometer reading at the faucet.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not clear the error, a technician should check the control board.

faq.q: Is the outlet sensor the same part as the inlet sensor?
faq.a: They may or may not be the same part number. Always order the correct sensor for the specific location.

faq.q: Can a sensor fail intermittently?
faq.a: Yes, a failing sensor can provide erratic readings before completely failing.

title: Heat Exchanger Outlet Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The secondary heat exchanger outlet temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription:
Error code 33 applies to condensing Rinnai models and indicates the secondary (condensing) heat exchanger outlet temperature sensor is malfunctioning. This sensor monitors the temperature at the outlet of the secondary heat exchanger where additional heat is extracted from exhaust gases.

The secondary heat exchanger operates at lower temperatures than the primary and deals with condensate. The sensor in this area can be affected by moisture and acidic condensate, leading to corrosion and premature failure.

This error only appears on condensing models and does not apply to standard non-condensing Rinnai units.

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the secondary heat exchanger sensor connector is secure and free of corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. Check the service manual for the expected value at current temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor reads out of range, replace it with an OEM part designed for the condensing heat exchanger.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not clear the error, a technician should check for condensate-related damage.

faq.q: What is the secondary heat exchanger?
faq.a: In condensing models, it extracts additional heat from exhaust gases, achieving 95%+ efficiency.

faq.q: Why do sensors corrode more on condensing models?
faq.a: The condensing process creates acidic condensate that can accelerate corrosion of nearby components.

title: Combustion Air Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The combustion air temperature sensor has failed or is reading out of range.
fullDescription:
Error code 34 indicates the sensor that measures the temperature of incoming combustion air has failed. This sensor helps the control board adjust the air-to-fuel ratio based on the density of incoming air, which changes with temperature.

Colder air is denser and requires different combustion tuning than warm air. Without accurate air temperature data, the unit cannot optimize combustion, which may lead to inefficiency or unsafe operation.

This sensor is typically located at the air intake of the unit and can be affected by dust, moisture, or extreme temperature exposure.
safetyWarning: Incorrect combustion air sensing can lead to improper air-fuel mixture and potentially elevated carbon monoxide. Ensure CO detectors are working.

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and check the air temperature sensor connector for a secure fit and corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and measure the sensor resistance. Compare to the service manual value for ambient temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the resistance is out of specification, replace the combustion air temperature sensor.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not resolve the error, the control board may need professional diagnosis.

faq.q: Where is the air temperature sensor?
faq.a: Typically at or near the combustion air intake on the unit.

faq.q: Is this sensor specific to my model?
faq.a: Yes, order by your Rinnai model number to ensure the correct part.

title: Freeze Protection Activated
shortDescription: The freeze protection system has activated due to low temperatures.
fullDescription:
Error code 41 indicates the Rinnai unit's freeze protection system has activated. Rinnai tankless water heaters include built-in freeze protection that uses internal heaters to prevent water from freezing inside the heat exchanger.

This code appears when the internal temperature drops to the freeze protection activation point (typically around 37 degrees Fahrenheit). The unit's heaters activate to keep the internal water above freezing.

Freeze protection only works when the unit has electrical power. If power is lost during freezing conditions, the unit can suffer freeze damage, which is not typically covered by warranty.

Note that freeze protection only protects the unit itself, not the external water pipes.
safetyWarning: If the unit loses power during freezing temperatures, drain the unit immediately to prevent freeze damage, which can crack the heat exchanger.

causes.cause: Ambient temperature near the unit dropped below threshold
Unit installed in an unprotected location

symptoms: Hear unit activating in cold weather
Power consumption increases in cold weather

fixSteps.title: Verify Ambient Temperature
fixSteps.instruction: Check the temperature in the area where the unit is installed. If it is near freezing, the activation is normal.

fixSteps.title: Insulate the Area
fixSteps.instruction: If the unit is in an unheated space, add insulation or provide supplemental heating to keep the area above 40 degrees Fahrenheit.

fixSteps.title: Check for Cold Air Infiltration
fixSteps.instruction: Verify that cold air is not entering through the venting system or gaps around the unit.

fixSteps.title: Ensure Power Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the unit has reliable power. Consider a UPS backup if power outages are common in your area during freezing weather.

whenToCallPro: If freeze protection activates in warm weather, the internal temperature sensor may be faulty.

faq.q: Does freeze protection work during a power outage?
faq.a: No. If power is lost during freezing weather, drain the unit to prevent damage.

faq.q: How do I drain my Rinnai for winter?
faq.a: Close the water valves, open hot water faucets, remove the inlet filter and drain plug to fully empty the unit.

title: Gas Modulation Valve Fault
shortDescription: The gas modulation valve is not responding correctly to control signals.
fullDescription:
Error code 52 indicates the proportional gas modulation valve is not operating within expected parameters. This valve controls the amount of gas flowing to the burner, allowing the unit to modulate heat output based on demand.

A failing gas modulation valve can cause the unit to fire at incorrect rates, leading to temperature fluctuations, overheating, or insufficient heating. The valve may be mechanically stuck or its electrical actuator may have failed.

This is a critical component for proper operation, and the unit will not function safely if the gas valve cannot be accurately controlled.
safetyWarning: A malfunctioning gas valve is a safety concern. If the unit produces erratic temperature or you smell gas, shut off the gas supply immediately.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the unit off, disconnect power for 60 seconds, then reconnect and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check Valve Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and inspect the gas valve electrical connections for loose or corroded contacts.

fixSteps.title: Listen for Valve Operation
fixSteps.instruction: When the unit attempts to fire, listen for the gas valve clicking. No click may indicate an electrical failure.

fixSteps.title: Professional Diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: Gas valve diagnosis and replacement must be performed by a licensed technician.

whenToCallPro: Immediately after confirming the wiring is intact. Gas valve repair and replacement must be done by a licensed professional.

faq.q: Can a gas valve be repaired?
faq.a: Typically no. Gas valves are replaced as complete assemblies for safety.

faq.q: How much does a Rinnai gas valve cost?
faq.a: Gas valve assemblies typically cost $150-$400 for the part, plus $100-$200 for professional installation.

title: Combustion Fan Failure
shortDescription: The combustion fan is not operating at the correct speed or has failed.
fullDescription:
Error code 61 on Rinnai tankless water heaters indicates the combustion fan (blower) is not functioning properly. The fan provides the precise amount of combustion air needed for safe and efficient operation.

The control board monitors fan speed through a tachometer signal. If the fan speed deviates significantly from the commanded speed, or if no tachometer signal is received, error 61 is triggered.

Fan failure can be caused by bearing wear, dust accumulation, an electrical motor failure, or a wiring issue. Proper fan operation is critical for combustion safety.
safetyWarning: Incorrect combustion air delivery can cause incomplete combustion and carbon monoxide production. Do not attempt to operate the unit if the fan is not working properly.

fixSteps.title: Listen to Fan
fixSteps.instruction: When the unit attempts to start, listen for abnormal sounds from the fan such as grinding, squealing, or rattling.

fixSteps.title: Check Fan Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify all fan motor connections are secure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Fan Blades
fixSteps.instruction: Check the fan blades for dust buildup or damage. Clean with a soft brush if dusty.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fan Motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is seized or making abnormal noise, the fan motor assembly must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If fan wiring checks out but the fan makes abnormal noise or does not spin, call a technician for motor replacement.

faq.q: How long do Rinnai fan motors last?
faq.a: Typically 8-15 years depending on environment and usage.

faq.q: Can I clean the fan myself?
faq.a: You can clean the visible fan blades, but motor disassembly should be done by a professional.

title: Water Flow Control Valve Fault
shortDescription: The water flow control valve is not operating correctly.
fullDescription:
Error code 65 indicates the water flow control valve (also called the water servo or bypass servo) is not responding properly to control signals. This valve adjusts the water flow rate through the heat exchanger to help maintain the set temperature.

When the desired temperature is lower than what the minimum burner firing rate would produce, the flow control valve increases water flow to compensate. If this valve fails, the unit cannot properly regulate temperature.

This error can also be caused by mineral deposits jamming the valve, a failed actuator motor, or a wiring issue.
symptoms: Water temperature too hot or too cold

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the unit off, wait 30 seconds, and restart. The valve may re-initialize to its correct position.

fixSteps.title: Check Valve Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and verify the flow control valve electrical connector is secure.

fixSteps.title: Descale the Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a full descaling flush which may free mineral deposits jamming the flow control valve.

fixSteps.title: Replace Valve Assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve actuator has failed, the flow control valve assembly needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If descaling and power cycling do not resolve the error, a technician should diagnose and replace the flow control valve.

faq.q: What does the flow control valve do?
faq.a: It adjusts water flow through the heat exchanger to help maintain the set temperature, especially at low flow rates.

faq.q: Can descaling fix the flow control valve?
faq.a: If scale is causing the valve to stick, descaling may free it. If the motor has failed, replacement is needed.
